title: Resolve Muzi R1/R1 Neo public schematic and board-revision gaps
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-ev5b.1 confirmed useful firmware sources but no official public schematic or board revision matrix for original R1/R1 Neo was found. What needs to be done: obtain vendor schematic/revision data or document that the Zephyr target is based on Meshtastic/MeshCore firmware pin metadata plus hardware smoke tests. Acceptance criteria: source of truth is recorded; any revision-specific pin or power differences have child Beads before hardware bring-up proceeds.
notes:
No-hardware resolution evidence (2026-07-01):

Current public-source search result:
- No official public schematic or board-revision matrix for Muzi Works R1 or R1 Neo was found in current public search. Checked Muzi public product/resources/user-guide pages, Muzi bootloader GitHub, Meshtastic hardware docs/releases, MeshCore issues/releases/variant references, and common external coverage.
- Muzi R1 Neo product/user-guide sources confirm product-level properties (built-in GPS, water-resistant/rugged device, multi-day battery, firmware flashing path) and document the open-source Adafruit nRF52-derived bootloader path, but do not publish a schematic or revision table.
- Muzi bootloader source confirms r1_neo_board as the bootloader/UF2 target and documents build/flash commands, but it is bootloader metadata, not a full hardware schematic.
- Meshtastic R1 Neo docs and firmware/release references identify R1 Neo as custom Muzi hardware and actively supported in Meshtastic, but public docs do not provide a schematic/revision matrix.
- MeshCore public issue/release references indicate R1 Neo support and identify nRF52840 + SX1262, but this remains firmware metadata/community support, not a vendor schematic.

Recorded source-of-truth policy:
- For the LICHEN Zephyr R1 Neo target, the source of truth is the source-backed firmware metadata already recorded in project-LICHEN-ev5b.1 plus LICHEN board DTS comments and follow-up smoke tests. Do not treat the R1 Neo Zephyr target as schematic-proven.
- The original non-Neo R1 is not aliased to the R1 Neo target. Its source-backed baseline remains RAK4631-class behavior plus Muzi R1 guide evidence; exact non-Neo pin/display/LR1121 boundaries remain separate from the R1 Neo MVP.
- Hardware bring-up may proceed only with explicit caveats already present in lichen/boards/muzi/r1_neo/r1_neo_nrf52840.dts: SX1262 reset is provisional, GNSS UART direction/control is disabled pending validation, UF2 partition layout needs bootloader validation, and battery/PMIC telemetry is not modeled as a HAL capability.

Revision-specific or pin/power follow-up coverage already exists:
- project-LICHEN-ev5b.9: validate R1 Neo SX1262 reset pin behavior before final overlay.
- project-LICHEN-ev5b.10: validate Muzi/Adafruit bootloader partition and UF2 layout on hardware.
- project-LICHEN-ev5b.11: decide original non-Neo R1 display and possible LR1121 support boundary.
- project-LICHEN-63mu: validate R1 Neo GNSS UART direction and AG3335 controls.
- project-LICHEN-usqf: model R1 Neo battery ADC for HAL battery capability.
- project-LICHEN-t1v0: smoke-test R1 Neo power-hold GPIO timing.
- project-LICHEN-avrb/project-LICHEN-ev5b.5: provide physical R1 Neo/R1 access and run hardware flash/smoke validation.

No new child Bead was required from this pass because every concrete revision-specific pin, power, bootloader, GNSS, battery, or original-R1 boundary found in the evidence is already tracked by the Beads above. If Muzi later publishes an official schematic or revision table, update project-LICHEN-ev5b.1/hardware facts and reopen or supersede this policy as needed.

Source URLs checked/used:
- https://muzi.works/products/r1-neo-complete-meshtastic-device
- https://muzi.works/pages/r1-neo-user-guide
- https://muzi.works/pages/r1-user-guide
- https://muzi.works/pages/resources
- https://github.com/muzi-works/Adafruit_nRF52_Bootloader
- https://meshtastic.org/docs/hardware/devices/muziworks/r1-neo/
- https://meshtastic.org/docs/hardware/devices/muziworks/
- https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/issues/1275
- https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/releases
- https://linuxgizmos.com/r1-neo-meshtastic-device-introduced-with-gps-and-nrf52840-processor/
- https://www.electronics-lab.com/muzi-works-r1-neo-meshtastic-device-with-dual-gps-ip68-and-lora-connectivity/
Review evidence (2026-07-01):
- Confucius reported no P0/P1/P2; verified source-of-truth policy is recorded, R1 Neo is not treated as schematic-proven, and follow-up Beads exist for concrete revision/pin/power areas. Checked DTS caveats for R1 Neo-only scope, provisional SX1262 reset, disabled GNSS UART, and UF2 partition validation.
- Dewey independently reported no P0/P1/P2 and closure acceptable; verified public schematic/revision absence is documented, firmware-metadata-plus-smoke-tests is adequate for no-hardware source-of-truth, board files reflect caveats, and follow-up Beads cover reset, GNSS, battery/PMIC, power-hold, bootloader/partition, original non-Neo R1 boundaries, and physical flash/smoke validation.

Verification:
- Web/public-source search performed on 2026-07-01 for Muzi Works R1/R1 Neo schematic/revision data, Muzi product/resources/user guides, Muzi bootloader GitHub, Meshtastic hardware docs/releases, MeshCore issue/release references, and external coverage. No public schematic/revision matrix found.
- git diff --check: PASS.
- No code changes, Zephyr build, or EC2 run required for this evidence-only no-hardware resolution.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware resolution: no public schematic/revision matrix found; source-of-truth policy recorded as firmware metadata plus DTS caveats and hardware smoke follow-ups; existing child Beads cover revision/pin/power gaps; two reviews clean.
